records.ety: [L. imprudentia: cf. F. imprudence. Cf. Improvidence.]

defs: The quality or state of being imprudent; want to caution, circumspection, or a due regard to consequences; indiscretion; inconsideration; rashness; also, an imprudent act; as, he was guilty of an imprudence.
quotes: His serenity was interrupted, perhaps, by his own imprudence.
